Based on the extensive review of literature, it has become evident that positive spinal reflexes persist even in cases of brain death. This persistence does not contradict the overall criteria for determining brain death because spinal reflexes operate independently from higher cortical centers. They continue functioning until there is significant degradation in spinal cord integrity, which would manifest differently from simply positive spinal reflexes alone.
